items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

gallery.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

thumbnail.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: MP joins conservative Family First party ahead of abortion bill debate

children.content: SA upper house MP Sarah Game has abandoned the political party she founded to join the conservative Family First brand, marking the third time she has changed parties in less than two years.

image.altText: Sarah Game smiles outside parliament house

items.title: SA Electoral Commissioner resigns amid review into March poll

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry has resigned amid a review into the scandal-plagued state and Voice elections.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie against a colourful background.

items.title: 'Reasonable possibility' father injected insulin himself, murder trial jury told

children.content: A woman standing trial accused of murdering her parents with insulin has denied the allegations, while her defence lawyer has told the jury the possibility one of the alleged victims injected a fatal dose of insulin himself cannot be ruled out.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

items.title: Man arrested after allegedly assaulting four cops, biting two, during arrest

children.content: A man has been arrested after he allegedly assaulted a woman and a group of police officers in regional South Australia.

image.altText: A red brick building with police cars parked in front of it

items.title: 'Ecosystem engineers' returned to desert decades after local extinction

children.content: Wiped off mainland Australia by the introduction of cats and foxes, burrowing bettongs are making a triumphant comeback in outback NSW.

gallery.altText: A baby Burrowing Bettong being held in the hands of Dr Rebecca West at the Wild Deserts project
A burrowing bettong jumps out of a sack in outback NSW.
A Crest-tailed Mulgara being held by Dr Rebecca West at Wild Deserts

thumbnail.altText: A baby Burrowing Bettong being held in the hands of Dr Rebecca West at the Wild Deserts project
A burrowing bettong jumps out of a sack in outback NSW.
A Crest-tailed Mulgara being held by Dr Rebecca West at Wild Deserts

items.title: Two more SA hospitals pause birthing services amid staff shortage

children.content: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

image.altText: A hospital building with a ramp leading to the entrance and a white roof

items.title: Rabbits are everywhere in the wild, so why is rabbit meat so expensive?

children.content: Once a cheap meat for families doing it tough, the price and availability of rabbit means it has gone from family dinner to fine dining.

image.altText: A rabbit in a paddock.

items.title: 'Smiling since last night': Community joy after Irankunda's World Cup goal

children.content: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

gallery.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

thumbnail.altText: Two soccer players in green and gold celebrate a goal

items.title: Cancer patient in remission after 'amazing' outcome of targeted trial

children.content: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

image.altText: Profile of a man sitting on a bench.

items.title: Algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom

children.content: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

image.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

items.title: Feud erupts as consumers on the hook for billion-dollar grid blowout

children.content: Transgrid wants to claw back huge cost increases for a major transmission line from consumers. Experts say the regulator's decision will have far-reaching consequences.

image.altText: Men working to construct a large transmission line tower in southern NSW

items.title: Accused murderer 'poisoned parents to death' with insulin, court hears

children.content: Adelaide woman Raelene Polymiadis is standing trial for two counts of murder after allegedly deliberately administering "fatal doses of insulin" to her parents in separate incidents, just over a year apart.

image.altText: A woman wearing sunglasses walks along a footpath with a man.

items.title: Iran's players celebrated at World Cup but complicated feelings remain

children.content: After a challenging, politically charged build-up to their World Cup campaign Iran's 2-2 draw with New Zealand brought mixed feelings for the Iranian diaspora.

articles.title: Quick hits: Cabo Verde finds player on LinkedIn, meet Merlin the Duck

children.content: Cabo Verde's incredible result against Spain brings with it no shortage of amazing stories, while a duck in a jersey is stealing hearts in Mexico. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

image.altText: Day five quick hits art

articles.title: New Zealand denied maiden win as Iranians protest outside stadium

children.content: Protesters demonstrate outside the stadium as Iran opens its World Cup with a 2-2 draw against New Zealand, ending a day of stalemates.

image.altText: Elijah Just celebrates a goal by holding his hands up to his eyes like binoculars

articles.title: Australian ref explains gesture after World Cup monitor calls for sacking

children.content: The Australian referee at the centre of a controversy around an alleged hate symbol being used during the World Cup broadcast has responded.

image.altText: Shaun Evans at the World Cup in referee uniform

gallery.altText: Iran player Ramin Rezaeian smiles and holds arms outstretched after scoring a goal
Iranian fans celebrate in the background as players embrace after a goal is scored
A woman draped in an Iranian 'Lion and Sun' flag yells at a protest

thumbnail.altText: Iran player Ramin Rezaeian smiles and holds arms outstretched after scoring a goal
Iranian fans celebrate in the background as players embrace after a goal is scored

items.title: Dog rescued after being trapped for five days in breakwater

children.content: A 14-year-old fox terrier is back in her owner's arms after a multi-day stint trapped in the Glenelg breakwater after she broke free from her leash.

image.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a blue shirt and vest smiling and cuddling a small black, white and brown dog.

items.title: Lancaster bomber pilot and Riverland volunteer Howard Hendrick remembered

children.content: Howard Hendrick's life was dedicated to service in the Royal Australian Air Force and to his community. His contributions will forever remembered by his family and his hometown.

image.altText: Framed photos are stacked on top of each other showing a man flying a plane and getting married.

items.title: Charges dropped against 'masked man' after fire at popular tourism site

children.content: A man who was charged after a fire near Mount Gambier will still have to appear in court to face harm, theft and offensive language charges.

image.altText: Flames on the side of a road behind a guardrail on a sunny day

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

children.content: Research suggests many under-16s are still active on social media platforms. BTN High explores why and tests the accuracy of popular technology used to estimate age.

image.altText: A teenage boy holds up a mobile phone with the number 16 on it. Logos for Facebook, X and other platforms swirl near his face.

items.title: Artemis III pilot Luca Parmitano recalls witnessing bushfires from space

children.content: Luca Parmitano was commanding the International Space Station when bushfires devastated eastern Australia. He has a blunt warning for the future of the planet.

gallery.altText: Astronaut in space suit facing the camera floating in space
Satellite image taken from space of bushfires in Australia.
Four men in NASA uniforms wave to a crowd.

thumbnail.altText: Astronaut in space suit facing the camera floating in space
Satellite image taken from space of bushfires in Australia.
Four men in NASA uniforms wave to a crowd.

items.title: SA Police charge man over alleged possession of mobile phone gun

children.content: South Australian police have separately arrested two men for the alleged possession of a handgun disguised as a mobile phone, and a homemade "pen gun".

image.altText: A red mobile phone-looking item split in half to reveal a black trigger in the middle of the top half.

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

children.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

image.altText: An overhead view of a BHP wellfield in outback South Australia.

items.title: 'Lives in danger' as SA Bureau of Meteorology wave buoy fails yet again

children.content: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

image.altText: An orange wave buoy rider in the ocean

items.title: Pauline Hanson to address National Press Club for first time

children.content: Pauline Hanson will speak at the National Press Club for the first time since her entrance to politics in 1996. Follow live.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson and Cory Bernardi 2026-05-05 09:05:00

items.title: After the first attempt failed, outback town gives renewables another go

children.content: This outback town is giving renewable energy another shot as a new solar farm and battery system hooks up to the grid, two decades after a solar array project failed to live up to the hype.

gallery.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

thumbnail.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

items.title: ASX slips, oil plunges to three-month low on reports US to scale back Iran sanctions

children.content: Oil prices sink to a three-month low on reports the US will allow Iran to start selling oil again, while the Australian share market falls after a sell-off across Wall Street's tech and AI stocks. Follow live.

image.altText: The ASX building exterior.

items.title: Advocates call for a rethink on the factors driving male suicide

children.content: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

image.altText: close uup of Paul Withall

items.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

gallery.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
About 10 white cockatoos sit on wooden table tops and seats outside a cafe.

Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
About 10 white cockatoos sit on wooden table tops and seats outside a cafe.

items.title: Six gaming experts share their favourite video games of the year so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: What happened when Ann Patchett quit swearing for good

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

image.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: Police say man thankful after drug arrest halts lethal mushroom mix-up

children.content: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

image.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.

items.title: The councillor, the tobacco shop and a booming black market

children.content: A councillor from the Upper Hunter in NSW manages a tobacco shop that sells illegal cigarettes as experts warn the black-market crisis is deepening in regional areas.

image.altText: A board featuring a bearded man and the words "vote 1 for councillor" outside a shop.

items.title: 'Unfair on him': Origin coaches back ref after gambling history revealed

children.content: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

articles.title: State of Origin quiz: Tackle our More or Less challenge ahead of Game II

children.content: Today's ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to go deep into AFL territory and come away with the win. Mouthguards in, let's go.

image.altText: New South Wales and Queensland players fight

articles.title: What time is State of Origin Game II tonight?

children.content: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

image.altText: Reuben Cotter of the Maroons is tackled by Isaah Yeo and Hudson Young of the Blues

gallery.altText: An referee points at a player to leave the field.

items.title: Qld government accused of campaign to remove Aboriginal people from health roles

children.content: The Queensland government will not deny the existence of "Project Invisibility", a name a barrister says is used internally to refer to the deliberate removal of Aboriginal people from leadership roles.

image.altText: An Indigenous man speaking while standing in front of Aboriginal and Australian flags.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Women forced to labour in tearoom, run-down wards at NT's busiest hospital

children.content: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, holding a baby who is facing away while she looks right at the camera.
